title: SPECTROPHOTOMETRIC AND CONDUCTOMETRIC STUDIES OF METAL COMPLEXES OF 3-HYDROXY-I-p-SULPHONATOPHENYL-3-PHENYL-  TRIAZINE. PART I. PALLADIUM COMPLEX
author: UMESH CHANDRA DURGAPAL
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Palladium forms an intensely yellow-coloured, water-soluble complex with 3-hydroxy-I-p-sulphona­tophenyl-3-phenyltriazine. The formula of the complex, established spectrophotometrically, by applying Job's method, slope ratio method and molar ratio method and also conducto­metrically, has been determined to be PdR 2  (R=reagent) The molecular extinction coefficient has been found to be 18.75 x 10 3  at 425mμ. The dissociation constant at 25 °  has been found to be 3 x 10 -12   and free energy of formation of the complex is -15.72Kcal/mole.
steps_raw: Palladium forms an intensely yellow-coloured, water-soluble complex with 3-hydroxy-I-p-sulphona­tophenyl-3-phenyltriazine. The dissociation constant at 25 °  has been found to be 3 x 10 -12   and free energy of formation of the complex is -15.72Kcal/mole.
